note:
[编辑2019年4月25日：请注意，这不是iPhone或Android应用程序，我没有计划这样发布。你可以使用手机或任何其他相机来拍摄磨碎的咖啡，但随后你需要在OSX上或通过Python(在任何操作系统上)安装该应用程序来分析数据。单击此处下载应用程序包。]。
今天，我想介绍一个我已经开发了几个月的OSX应用程序。事实证明，在为天体物理学编写了一天的Python软件之后，为咖啡编写Python软件是一种很好的放松方式。
几年前，当我开始对冲泡特色咖啡感兴趣时，最让我恼火的事情之一是，我们无法为不同的咖啡冲泡方法推荐研磨机的大小，或者客观地比较不同研磨机的质量。当然，一些实验室有激光衍射设备，可以测量从研磨机出来的所有颗粒的大小，但很少有咖啡极客能够接触到这些价值数十万美元的设备。
起初，我决定将我的咖啡渣铺在一张白纸上拍照，并使用一款名为ImageJ的旧软件，该软件由美国国立卫生研究院(National Institutes Of Health)开发，主要用于分析显微镜图像，以获得我的咖啡渣的大小分布。这很好地工作，让我可以开始比较不同的磨床。然后Scott Rao让我意识到，不需要复杂安装并且专门用于咖啡的独立应用程序将会引起咖啡业的许多人的兴趣。可能只有10%的极客，但这很酷。
我希望这个应用程序能帮助我们理解粒度分布对咖啡味道的影响。我不认为该行业真的让我们了解所有的激光衍射实验，所以希望我们作为一个社区可以帮助自己。
如果您对测量研磨机的粒度分布感兴趣，那么此应用程序是为您准备的-而且是免费的。我把它作为“开源”放在GitHub上，所以如果你是一名开发人员，欢迎你以推送请求的形式向我发送建议(开发人员会知道这是什么意思)。
如果你想开始，我建议你阅读这个快速安装指南，它将解释如何下载和运行应用程序，即使我不是注册的苹果开发者。然后，您可以选择阅读这份让您掌握基本知识的快速摘要，也可以选择阅读这本非常详细和冗长的用户手册，它将指导您了解应用程序提供的所有详细选项。
我想向您展示一个使用该软件可以做些什么的例子。下面，我比较了使用54 mm扁钢毛刺的Baratza Forté磨床和使用48 mm圆锥钢毛刺的Lido 3手动磨床的粒度分布。我设置两台研磨机的方式是产生直径在1毫米左右的平均大小颗粒的相似峰值，但正如您所看到的，颗粒大小分布非常不同！Forté产生的罚款(直径小于0.5毫米)和巨石(直径约为2毫米)要少得多，这表明磨床的质量更好。
目前，这款应用程序只打算在OS X电脑上使用。但是，如果您正在运行任何其他类型的系统，并且熟悉Python，您总是可以直接从GitHub下载它，并在您自己安装的Python3中运行它。
我要感谢Scott Rao在我与他分享这个项目想法时的兴奋，以及对该软件的Beta测试。我还要感谢Alex Levitt、Mitch Hale、Caleb Fischer、Francisco Quijano和Victor Malherbe对软件进行了Beta测试。
note_en:
[Edit April 25 2019: Please note  this is not an iPhone or Android app, and I have no plans to release it as such. You can use your phone or any other camera to take pictures of your ground coffee, but then you need to install the application on either OS X or through Python (on any operating system) to analyze the data.  Download the application package here.]
 Today I would like to present an OS X application I have been developing for a few months. It turns out writing Python software for coffee is a great way to relax after a day of writing Python software for astrophysics.
 When I started being interested in brewing specialty coffee a few years ago, one of the first things that irritated me was our inability to recommend grind sizes for different coffee brewing methods, or to compare the quality of different grinders in an objective way. Sure, some laboratories have laser diffraction equipment that can measure the size of all particles coming out of a grinder, but rare are the coffee geeks that have access to these multi-hundred thousands of dollars kinds of equipment.
 At first, I decided to take pictures of my coffee grounds spread on a white sheet, and to use an old piece of software called  ImageJ, developed by the National Institutes of Health mainly to analyze microscope images, to obtain a distribution of the sizes of my coffee grounds. This worked decently well, and allowed me to start comparing different grinders. Then  Scott Rao made me realize that a stand-alone application that doesn’t need a complicated installation and that is dedicated to coffee would be of interest to many people in the coffee industry. Probably just the 10% geekiest of them, but that’s cool.
 I’m hoping that this application will help us understand the effects of particle size distributions on the taste of coffee. I don’t think the industry really kept us in the loop with all the laser diffraction experiments, so hopefully we can help ourselves as a community.
 If you are interested in measuring the particle size distribution of your grinder, then this app is for you ‒ and it’s free. I placed it as “open source” on  GitHub, so if you are a developer, you are welcome to send me suggestions in the form of  push requests (the developers will know what that means).
 If you would like to get started, I suggest you read this quick  installation guide, which will explain how to download the app and run it even though I am not a registered Apple Developer. Then, you can choose to either read this  quick summary that will get you running with the basics, or this   very detailed and wordy  user manual that will guide you through all the detailed options the application offers you.
 I would like to show you an example of what can be done with the software. Below, I am comparing the particle size distribution of the Baratza Forté grinder, which uses 54 mm flat steel burrs, with that of the Lido 3 hand grinder, which uses 48 mm conical steel burrs. I set both grinders in a way that produces a similar peak of average-sized particles with diameters around 1 mm, but as you can see, the particle size distributions are very different ! The Forté generates way less fines (with diameters below 0.5 mm) and slightly less boulders (with diameters of approximately 2 mm), which is indicative of a better quality grinder.
  For now, the app is only intended to be used on OS X computers. But if you are running any other kind of system and know your way around Python, you can always download it directly from  GitHub and run it with your own installation of Python 3.
  I would like to thank Scott Rao for his excitement when I shared this project idea with him, and for beta testing the software. I would also like to thank Alex Levitt, Mitch Hale, Caleb Fischer, Francisco Quijano and Victor Malherbe for beta testing the software.
title: 一款测量咖啡研磨粒度分布的应用程序
title_en: An App to Measure Your Coffee Grind Size Distribution

note_en: Thiswebpage describes the MIOS Project. MIOS is achip-for-chip replacement of the BIOS (Basic Input Output System) onthe IBM 5150 Personal Computer. On the IBM PC the BIOS is contained ina ROM IC Chip located on the motherboard at socket location U33. The ICis socketed and can be replaced with a custom ROM containingcustom code.    The purpose of this project is to explore controlling the IBM PChardware in non-standard ways. The purpose is not to replace the BIOSwith another BIOS that does exactly the same thing! We are going todescribe how MIOS works by describing the path we took for development.   We started this project by considering other BIOS replacement software.Notably for the IBM PC there is a diagnostic ROM replacement known asthe Landmark/Supersoft package, as described here:  MinusZero Degrees    The Landmark/Supersoft code is unique. By burning the codeto ROM and placing in BIOS location U33 it is possible to boot the IBMPC and perform a series of diagnostic tests, with the resultsreported on the screen in real time. A photo of a typical displayscreen is shown in Figure 1.   Thekey point about thediagnosticROM is the fact the code is completely controlling the PChardware: There is no DOS operating system. There is no BIOS.          Controlling PC hardware directly, without using the standard BIOS, isfacilitated by the extensive available documentation describinghow the IBM 5150works and operates. The IBM Technical Manual contains the completecircuit diagrams, with useful additional descriptions of hardwarecomponents, and a complete listing of the BIOS source code. Theassembly code control of computer hardware without a BIOSor operating system is sometimes referred to as using "primitives".Direct control of computer hardware has certain advantages, not theleast ofwhich is the possibly of increased operating speed.    As built, the IBM 5150 PC dedicated a single ROM socket for the BIOS.This is socket U33 on the motherboard, as shown in Figure 2. The socketis designed to accepta Motorola MC68766 programmable ROM. The ROM has space for8192bytes of code. (This is often abbreviated as "8K".) The original ROMchip chosen by IBM is comparatively rare, and the programming protocolrequires vintage hardware that can be difficult to find.Fortunately, the ROM chip can be substituted with a much more common ICchip by using a socket adapter. The MIOS code must be burned into a ROMchip before it can be used. We use an adapter with theprogrammable M27128Achip,as shown in Figure 3.          MIOS has been designed to avoid the use of CPUinterrupts. This marks a major departure from the design of theoriginal IBM 5150 BIOS. We decided to pursue this design approach basedupon a similar idea as presented by  PerBrinchHansen, in his book "Programminga Personal Computer", 1982, P. 54.The first task of the MIOS software then, is to elimate interrupts tothe 8088 CPU. Interrupts to the CPU have multiple sources: Maskableexternal interrupts, non-maskable external interrupts, and non-maskableinternal interrupts.           The maskable interrupts can be masked by using the assembly codecommand "CLI", a mneumonic with the meaning "CLear Interrupts". As luckwould have it, the IBM 5150 external non-maskable interrupts can indeedbydisabled with a command to a logic gate preceding the CPU input pin, asdescribed in Mazidi, P. 435.  (Masking NMIInterrupts) The internal non-maskable interrupts arethe only remaining. (Interrupt 0: Divide byZero, etc)     The 8088 CPU iscodedinternally to proceed to a specific memory address at the start of theRAM to read an interrupt vector address to follow and execute code tohandle the interrupt. The first 16K of RAM in the IBM PC is soldereddirectly to the motherboard. Although this RAM is available for use,since it is dynamic RAM, the memory requires constant refreshing tohold address byte content. Therefore we do not have an easy way tohandle the non-maskable internal CPU interrupts. We recognize this as adeficiency in MIOS. Here is a discussion of operating without DRAMrefresh interrupts.  (DRAMRefresh)   MIOS is designed to operate in conjunction with an MDA display adapter.The MDA is the simplest display adapter for the IBM 5150 PC. However,the MDAdisplay adapter has one very notable and useful feature: The videomemory is SRAM, not RAM.  The SRAM on the MDA card can be usedbyMIOS as general-purpose memory for creating a Stack Segment (SS) andExtra Segment (ES). Using SRAM avoids the complexity and resourcerequirements of servicing an interrupt for memory refresh.          Before the MDA display adapter can be used it must be initialized. TheMDA is initialized by sending setup byte parameters to onboard memorylocations called registers. Thisturns out to be fairly easy to do because of excellent documentionregards the inner workings of the MDA:  MDACard Explanation   We were fortunate to also find a coding example for initializing theMDA registers using assembly code. Although we did not use the codedirectly, the basic outline in MIOS is very similar.  MDACardInitializaton Example   Working with the ROM code space of  8192 bytes is accomplishedbyusing specialized software called a Hex Editor. It is akin to a texteditor, but optimized for editing hexadecimal machine codeinstructions. Our favorite hex editor is HxD by Mael Horz, availablehere:  HxDHex Editor           With a hex editor you can directly view and manipulate the 8192 bytesof code for the MIOS ROM. A typical screenshot of a hex editing sessionis shown in the Figure 5. The ROM space at motherboard location U33 ishardwired to map to the 8088 memory space as locations FE00:0000 toFF00:FFFF. (These addresses are represented in characteristicSegment:Offset notation. See an explanation at  Segment:OffsetExplanation) This memory is atthe very top of thedirectly-addressable memory in the IBM 5150 PC.   The MIOS input/output operating system is coded in 8086 assembly code.We have opted to code and compile using a software tool characteristicof the time period. We are using Microsoft Assembler 2.0 ("MASM 2.0").We use MASM to compile and link the code, and the "EXE2BIN.EXE utilityto "flatten" the executable to a simple binary file. (Basically toremove the Program Segment Prefix (PSP).) Here is a link to historicalinformation for MASM:  MASMVersion History          When power is applied, the 8088 CPU is internally programmed tojumpto a specific memory location for its first machine code instruction.That first instruction is expected to reside at memory location:FF00:FFF0. This address corresponds to the beginning of the last 16byte line in the U33 memory space. In the original IBM BIOS thislocation contains an unconditional jump to the start of the boot code.MIOS does the same thing, with an unconditional jump to thefirst byte of the ROM at: FE00:0000.         The IBM PC uses a segmented memory system. Briefly, thememory is oftenorganized into 4 main segments: CS: Code Segment is a sequential list of machine code instructions forthe CPU. DS: Data Space is a section of byte-based data the CPU can reference. SS: Stack Segment is a temporary memory storage area, acessed usingPUSH and POP instructions. ES: Extra Segment is a destination area for displayinginformation, often the video monitor.    MIOS is written to interface directly with the IBM PC Keyboard. Thekeyboard connects to the motherboard using a 5 Pin DIN connector. Wefound a good assembly code example of a keyboard hardware interface inSam's Photofact Book:  Keyboard ProgrammingExample    The interface routine we areusing for the keyboard is extremely basic. The keyboard sendsbyte-sized scancodes, which are different that the byte-sized ASCIIcodes representing a character. A translation must be performed. We dothe translation using the XLAT instruction with a data table. (XLAT =TRANSLATE). Another complication is that the keyboard actually sends 2scancodes per key tap:  1 scancode for press, and 1 scancode forrelease. The keyboard is polled for a keypress. Interrupts are not used.          As an experiment, we created a 64K version of MIOS for a 386motherboard as shown in Figure X. Because the motherboard has ISAexpansion slots, we were able to use the MDA video card. We weresurprised to discover that MIOS was able to boot the system. (Althoughthe keyboard interface did not work.) This experiment suggestsadditional possibilities for future development.          We have packaged the source code and binary files for MIOS in acompressed zip file. The file can be downloaded here:  MIOS.ZIP   Here is an short summary of the sequential steps taken in the MIOS code: 1) Unconditional jump to the first byte in the U33 ROM memory space. 2) Mask and disable interrupts. 3) Beep the speaker 4) Initialize the MDA card 5) Clear the screen 6) Send a POST code to show boot progress  (RequiresPOST card at 80HPort) 7) Define Data Segment, Extra Segment, Stack Segment 8) Display a welcome message ("MTM SCI") 9) Display a label in the Stack Segment ("STK") 10) Display 8088 CPU Registers 11) Poll the keyboard and display the ASCII character if a key ispressed          Here is a list of possible ideas for future improvements: 1) Programmed output on the parallet port on the MDA card.  (Speed Test Results) 2) Use the DIP switch settings for input or configuration. 3) Program one of the onboard timers to measure short programintervals. (Cycle Counter) 4) Add additional SRAM memory using an ISA card interface.  (PCSRAM Extension: SERGEY) 5) Create a dumb terminal using RS-232 I/O. ( SerialPort Card and the  Initialize Code) 6) Add more features to the diagnostic interface. (Something like DEBUG) 7) Interface to a RTC card in an ISA slot. Poll the RTC registers fortimekeeping. 8) Interface to an 8087 FPU (Math Coprocessor) 9) Add hardware tests and diagnostics, with a progress display 10) Interface to CGA Video. Explore initializing a VGA card using theregisters.   We consulted a large number of books and references developing the MIOScode. Especially useful were the following books. The book by Mazidiwas especially helpful, and can be found on EBAY for less than $10.
title: MIOS：IBM 5150 BIOS更换项目

note:
研究人员透露，英国冠状病毒抗体携带者的比例在三个月内下降了四分之一以上，这加剧了人们对再次感染的担忧。
这一发现来自Reaction-2研究，该研究基于来自所有314个地方当局的随机参与者的家庭指刺抗体测试结果。
第一个结果是基于10万人的数据，于8月份发布，显示约6%的英格兰人口拥有抗体-感染时产生的保护性蛋白-尽管研究小组表示，这可能略有低估。
这项尚未经过同行审查的新工作扩展了这一点，在两个新的队列中进行了更多的测试，每个队列产生了来自超过10万名成年人的结果。
结果显示，在9月15日至28日的最近一轮检测中，只有4.4%的人有可检测到的冠状病毒抗体。
该报告的合著者、伦敦帝国理工学院传染病教授格雷厄姆·库克(Graham Cooke)表示：“总体而言，这些数据表明，随着可检测到的抗体在人群中下降，人群免疫力下降将导致再次感染风险增加的可能性。”
研究人员说，抗体预计会随着时间的推移而下降，但这需要多长时间，水平下降的程度因病毒而异-当涉及到保护持续多长时间时，这些因素是很重要的。
伦敦帝国理工学院流感病毒学主席、该报告的合著者温迪·巴克利教授表示，目前还不清楚需要什么水平的抗体才能保护某人免受新冠肺炎的感染或再次感染，但实验表明，抗体水平可能与抗体测试的检测阈值大致相同。
一些人提出了这样一种可能性，即即使抗体已经减弱，免疫系统的其他组成部分也可能继续提供保护-比如可以杀死感染细胞的T细胞，或者可以迅速产生新抗体的B记忆细胞。然而，该研究小组表示，现在就知道情况是否如此，或者这种保护可能会持续多久还为时过早，因为很难测量这种T细胞的水平。
巴克利说：“人们在一生中定期再次感染季节性冠状病毒(导致普通感冒)，这表明这种免疫力，无论它是否由抗体介导和/或T细胞介导，都可能不会持续很长时间。”他补充说，研究小组怀疑身体对感染新冠状病毒的反应也是类似的。
但她说，新的结果并不一定意味着接种疫苗产生的免疫力将是短暂的。“一种好的疫苗很可能比自然免疫力更好。”
巴克莱补充说，如果真的发生第二次感染，免疫记忆可能意味着它不会像第一次那么严重。但是，她指出，目前缺乏数据：到目前为止，世界各地只有几例再次感染得到确认。
该团队表示，抗体仍然是预防再次感染的良好指标，并补充说，抗冠状病毒抗体的低流行率以及它们被证明下降的快速速度，削弱了在没有疫苗的情况下实施群体免疫政策的理由。
该研究的合著者、帝国理工学院公共卫生教授海伦·沃德(Helen Ward)表示：“当你认为每100个人中就有95个人不太可能免疫，因此很可能是易感人群，那么我们距离任何类似于防止继续传播的人群层面的保护措施都还有很长的路要走，”该研究的合著者海伦·沃德(Helen Ward)表示。
该团队表示，缺乏再感染可能是因为很难确认这类病例-但也可能是因为抗体水平没有经过足够的时间下降到大多数人容易再次感染的地步。
库克补充说，公众遵守Covid限制仍然很重要，同时也需要疫苗。他说：“这里的大局是，在第一波之后，这个国家的绝大多数人仍然没有保护性免疫的证据。”
note_en:
The proportion of people in England with coronavirus antibodies dropped by more than a quarter in the space of three months, researchers have revealed, fuelling concerns over reinfection.
 The findings come from  the React-2 study, which is based on home finger-prick antibody test results from random participants across all 314 local authorities.
 The first results, based on data from 100,000 people,  were released in August, revealing that about 6% of the population of England had the antibodies – protective proteins produced in response to an infection – although the team say that could be a slight underestimate.
 The new work – not yet peer-reviewed – extends this with more testing in two fresh cohorts, each yielding results from more than 100,000 adults.
 The results reveal that just 4.4% of those tested in the most recent round, between 15 and 28 September, had detectable coronavirus antibodies.
 “As a whole, these data suggest the possibility that decreasing population immunity will lead to an increased risk of reinfection as detectable antibodies decline in the population,” said Graham Cooke, co-author of the report and professor of infectious diseases at Imperial College London.
 The researchers said antibodies would be expected to decline over time, but just how long that takes, and how far levels fall, differ between viruses – factors that are important when it comes to how long protection lasts.
 Prof Wendy Barclay, chair of influenza virology at Imperial College London and co-author of the report, said it was not yet known what level of antibody is needed to protect someone from infection or reinfection with Covid-19, but experiments suggested it could be around the same level as the antibody test’s threshold of detection.
 Some have raised the possibility that other components of the immune system might continue to offer protection even if antibodies have waned – such as T-cells, which can kill infected cells, or B memory cells, which can rapidly produce new antibodies. However, the team said it is too soon to know if that is the case, or for how long that protection might last, while it is difficult to measure levels of such T-cells.
 “The fact that people get reinfected regularly throughout their lives with seasonal coronaviruses [that cause common colds] suggests that the immunity, whether or not it is antibody mediated and/or T-cell mediated, probably isn’t very long-lasting,” said Barclay, who added that the team suspect the body reacts to infection with the new coronavirus in a similar way.
 But she said the new results do not necessarily mean that immunity arising from vaccination would be short-lived. “A good vaccine may well be better than natural immunity.”
 Barclay added that if a second infection did occur, immunological memory might mean it would be less severe than the first. But, she noted, data is currently lacking: only a few cases of reinfection have, so far,  been confirmed around the world.
 The team said antibodies were still good indicator of protection against reinfections, adding that the low prevalence of antibodies against the coronavirus and the rapid pace at which they had been shown to fall undermine the case for a herd immunity policy without a vaccine.
 “When you think that 95 out of 100 people are unlikely to be immune and therefore likely to be susceptible, then we are a long, long way from anything resembling a population-level protection against onward transmission,” said co-author Helen Ward, professor of public health at Imperial.
 The team said the lack of reinfections could be because it is difficult to confirm such cases – but it may also be that not enough time has passed for antibody levels to have declined to the point where most people are susceptible to reinfection.
 Cooke added that it remains important that the public adheres to Covid restrictions, while a vaccine is also needed. “The big picture here is that after the first wave, still the great majority of the country didn’t have evidence of protective immunity,” he said.
title: 研究称，英国Covid抗体携带者的比例有所下降

note:
持怀疑态度的世界失去了舞台魔术师、避雷针和现代怀疑运动的联合创始人詹姆斯·兰迪(James Randi)这位杰出的人物。1976年，兰迪加入了哲学家保罗·库尔茨(Paul Kurtz)、天文学家卡尔·萨根(Carl Sagan)、心理学家雷·海曼(Ray Hyman)、科学作家马丁·加德纳(Martin Gardner)以及其他有动力的科学倡导者和边缘主张的批评者的行列，建立了北美第一个正式的怀疑论组织，当时被称为Csicop-超自然现象声称的科学调查委员会(现在称为CSI，怀疑论调查委员会)。
从20世纪70年代到21世纪头十年，兰迪可能是怀疑论史上最有影响力的人。他的经典之作“Flim-Flam！是科学怀疑论的定义文本。他开创了怀疑主义的许多调查技巧，以及怀疑主义活动家的许多论点和态度。虽然许多持怀疑态度的文学作品都是由历史侦探和批判性分析组成的，但兰迪以其独特的激进主义方式来对抗超自然和伪科学的主张而闻名。他对电视布道者彼得·波波夫(Peter Popoff)的戏剧性公开揭露，仍然是在实地收集的证据揭露边缘支持者的最明显的现代例子之一。大多数怀疑者会将兰迪描述为对他们自己的工作的强大鼓舞，包括我自己。
作为一名活动家和表演者，兰迪无疑是脱颖而出的。他采取了直言不讳、对抗性的公众形象，受到粉丝的喜爱和批评者的厌恶。萨根说，兰迪“准确地自称是一个愤怒的人”。科幻作家艾萨克·阿西莫夫(Isaac Asimov)写道：“兰迪反击，当伪科学家们嚎叫时，他知道自己击中了目标。”从1964年开始，兰迪向超自然索赔人发起了直接挑战：“在双方事先商定的适当观测条件下进行任何类型的超自然壮举”，并赢得1万美元。这笔奖金最终增长到100万美元，但没有人认领。虽然许多人都接受了测试，但从来没有人成功地展示过任何超自然的力量。
兰迪留下了丈夫和许多忠实的朋友。我不太了解兰迪，所以我会让他所爱的人来歌颂他这个人。相反，我被要求简要地考虑一下他作为怀疑论历史上的“开国元勋”留下的遗产。
这个问题很复杂，因为人很复杂。就像他之前的胡迪尼和巴纳姆一样，兰迪首先是一个表演者。他的公众形象比生活更重要，而在他职业生涯的大部分时间里，他的私生活基本上是不为人所知的。他精心设计和策划了自己的公众形象，从他选择的法定名字到他在81岁时选择出柜的同性恋身份。
他在公共舞台上的积极工作是他最明显的遗产。坦率地说，很难夸大他的工作对有组织的怀疑论的影响。兰迪对怀疑论的贡献是奠基的和不朽的。如果没有兰迪的能量和榜样，世界范围内的怀疑组织网络很可能永远不会发生。
与此同时，兰迪最伟大的贡献有时也会引起争议。例如，他的阿尔法计划派魔术师卧底进入一个超心理学实验室，在那里他们假扮成灵媒。当兰迪揭露真相时，实验室完全被愚弄了，并感到尴尬。同样，兰迪哄骗澳大利亚媒体对兰迪用整块布发明的假古鲁进行媒体报道。这些项目受到了怀疑者的广泛庆祝。他们还迫使怀疑者考虑一些问题，比如在我们的工作中使用欺骗的道德问题(怀疑者和我们的批评者至今仍在继续讨论)。
自胡迪尼时代以来，魔术师一直是对超自然现象进行必要的多学科研究的领导者和重要合作伙伴。几十年来，兰迪一直是杰出的持怀疑态度的魔术师。他用科学家和记者所缺乏的大量专业专家知识来处理超自然现象。例如，没有人比他更有资格揭露所谓的“心灵外科医生”的舞台魔术，他们为了钱而掠夺癌症患者。同样，兰迪也不是科学家。他有时在他不是专家的科学问题上说错话。(他对气候科学的怀疑导致我在2009年辩称，“怀疑论者可以负责任地涉足的科学论点是有限的”。)
我们如何开始权衡如此漫长而非凡的职业生涯呢？兰迪帮助创建了我热爱的运动。所有的怀疑论者都感谢他的工作。被兰迪揭穿从骗子手中解救出来的人也是如此。特别值得注意的是，他发起了反对心理手术和假炸弹探测装置的无情欺诈的运动。
随着我们继续兰迪开始的工作，他的工作将被讨论数十年。他是怀疑论最激烈、最引人注目、最有影响力的倡导者之一。没有他，这个持怀疑态度的项目就不会一样了。他退出了这个舞台，现在留给我们来决定这部剧必须如何继续下去。
在反思兰迪的成功揭穿时，萨根说：“依赖他揭露世界上所有的江湖医生、骗人和废话，就像相信那些江湖骗子一样危险。”(译者注：兰迪成功揭穿了兰迪，萨根说：“依赖他揭露世界上所有的庸医、骗人和废话，就像相信那些江湖骗子一样危险。”兰迪只是一个人。他不可能什么都做，也不可能永远做下去。然而，詹姆斯·兰迪和他的开拓性同事的工作确实改变了世界，至少改变了一点。他们把我们的注意力引向被主流社会忽视的紧迫问题。他们努力解决这些问题的努力为我们提供了学习、适应和拓展的榜样。接受这一挑战的责任落在我们身上。“如果我们不想被抓走，”萨根说，“我们需要为自己做这件事。”
note_en:
The skeptical world has lost a towering figure in James Randi, stage magician, lightning rod, and co-founder of the modern skeptical movement. In 1976, Randi joined philosopher Paul Kurtz, astronomer Carl Sagan, psychologist Ray Hyman, science writer Martin Gardner, and other motivated science advocates and critics of fringe claims to establish North America’s first formal skeptical organization, then known as CSICOP—the Committee for the Scientific Investigation of Claims of the Paranormal (now called CSI, the Committee for Skeptical Inquiry).
 Randi may be the single most influential person in the history of skepticism from the 1970s through the first decade of the 21 st century. His classic book  Flim-Flam! is a defining text for scientific skepticism. He pioneered many of skepticism’s investigative techniques, and many of the arguments and attitudes of skeptical activists. While much of the skeptical literature consists of historical sleuthing and critical analysis, Randi was known for his distinct activist approach to confronting paranormal and pseudoscientific claims. His dramatic public exposé of televangelist Peter Popoff remains one of the clearest modern examples of a fringe proponent unmasked by evidence gathered in the field. Most skeptics would describe Randi as a powerful inspiration for their own work, myself included.
 As an activist and showman, Randi certainly stood out. He adopted an outspoken, confrontational public persona beloved by his fans and detested by critics. Sagan said Randi was “accurately self-described as an angry man.” Science fiction author Isaac Asimov wrote that “Randi strikes back and when the pseudoscientists howl, he knows he has hit the mark.” Beginning in 1964, Randi trumpeted a direct challenge to paranormal claimants: “perform  any paranormal feat of  any kind under the proper observing conditions” mutually agreed in advance, and win $10,000. That prize eventually grew to $1 million, yet it went unclaimed. Though many were tested, no one ever succeeded in demonstrating any paranormal power whatsoever.
 Randi leaves behind a husband and many devoted friends. I did not know Randi well, so I will leave it to his loved ones to eulogize him as a person. I’ve been asked instead to briefly consider his legacy as a “founding father” within the history of skepticism.
 That question is complex because people are complex. Like Houdini and Barnum before him, Randi was a showman first. His public persona was larger than life, while his private life was largely unknown for most of his career. He crafted and curated his public persona, from his chosen legal name to his choice to come out as a gay man at age 81.
 His activist work on the public stage is his most visible legacy. It would frankly be difficult to overstate the impact of his work upon organized skepticism. Randi’s contributions to skepticism were foundational and monumental. It’s likely that the worldwide network of skeptical organizations would never have happened without Randi’s energy and example.
 At the same time, Randi’s greatest contributions were sometimes controversial. His Project Alpha, for example, sent magicians undercover into a parapsychology lab where they posed as psychics. The lab was fooled completely, and embarrassed when Randi revealed the truth. Similarly, Randi hoaxed Australian media into giving press coverage to a fake guru Randi had invented from whole cloth. These projects were widely celebrated by skeptics. They also forced skeptics to consider questions such as the ethics of using deception in our work (a discussion that skeptics and our critics continue to this day).
 Magicians have been leaders and essential partners in the necessarily multidisciplinary study of paranormal claims since the days of Houdini. For decades, Randi was the preeminent skeptical magician. He approached paranormal claims with a vast store of specialized expert knowledge that scientists and journalists lack. No one was better qualified to expose, for example, the stage magic trickery of so-called “psychic surgeons” who preyed upon cancer patients for money. By the same token, Randi was not a scientist. He sometimes misspoke on scientific questions in which he was not an expert. (His doubts regarding climate science led me to argue in 2009 that there are “limits on the kinds of scientific arguments into which skeptics may responsibly wade.”)
 How do we even begin to weigh such a long and extraordinary career? Randi helped to create the movement I love. All skeptics are indebted to his work. So too are the people rescued from swindlers by Randi’s debunking. Especially notable were his campaigns against the heartless frauds of psychic surgery and fake bomb detecting devices.
 His work will be discussed for decades as we continue what Randi began. He was one of skepticism’s fiercest, most visible, and most influential advocates. The skeptical project will not be the same without him. His exit from this stage leaves us now to decide how the show must go on.
 Reflecting on Randi’s successful debunking, Sagan said, “it would be as dangerous to rely on him to expose all the quacks, humbugs, and bunkum in the world as it would be to believe those same charlatans.” Randi was one man. He couldn’t do everything, and he couldn’t do it forever. And yet, the work of James Randi and his pioneering colleagues actually did change the world, at least a little bit. They directed our notice to urgent problems ignored by mainstream society. Their attempts to grapple with those problems provide examples for us to learn from, adapt, and expand. It falls on us to accept that challenge. “If we don’t want to get taken,” Sagan said, “we need to do this job for ourselves.”

当克拉拉考虑平衡她作为性工作者的事业和作为护生的生活时，她试图引导迪士尼角色汉娜·蒙塔娜(Hannah Montana)。
在该剧中，主人公麦莉·斯图尔特看起来像一个普通的青少年，但她拥有流行歌星汉娜·蒙塔娜的双重生活，克拉拉表示，保持自己的身份分离是关键。
“汉娜·蒙塔娜(Hannah Montana)，”20岁的克拉拉(Clara)说，出于对歧视的担忧，她要求不透露自己的姓氏。“两全其美，但你要把它们分开。你不能把工作和娱乐混为一谈。“。
在佛罗里达上大学的克拉拉在大学医院担任病人护理助理，直到她说至少有十几名护士感染了冠状病毒，医院更换了人员，导致克拉拉失业。由于没有工资和账单要付，她决定在OnlyFans上开设一个账户，这是一个以自制色情作品闻名的网站。对于克拉拉来说，这几个月是忙碌的几个月，即使她脑海中有一个电视角色，她说管理这一切仍然是一个挑战。
“我觉得我拿着两磅橘子，被蒙上眼睛，走在钢丝上……。不管我去哪里，我要么向右倒下，要么向左倒下，但我必须一直往前走，“她说。
克拉拉是第一代美国人，来自秘鲁的移民的女儿，她决心用她所说的上帝赋予她的技能-聪明的头脑，努力工作的能力，以及与人相处的随和-让她的父母感到自豪，并向人们展示她要去某个地方。性工作不是她计划的一部分，但只要她能抓住橙子，它就能帮助她保持在通往她想去的地方的道路上。
克拉拉说：“(我是那种认为全世界都在看的人)，在某种程度上，我和Fans一起做的事情是真的。”“是未来让我沮丧，然后是期望，确保我不会让任何人失望。现在会发生什么呢？你知道的。“。
年轻女性通过性工作支付学费的故事并不是什么新鲜事，但自从世卫组织宣布冠状病毒是一种流行病以来的七个月里，网络性工作-通常被排除在打车和送餐应用的讨论之外-已经成为零工经济越来越主流的一个方面，像克拉拉这样的人表示，为了维持自己的生存，风险是值得的。
“说到底，没人帮我付房租，没人付我的车险、电话费、猫粮。没什么，“她说。“所以我必须这么做。这对我们很有帮助。“。
今年春天，当全国各地的城市每晚都在欢呼庆祝医护人员成为英雄时，米歇尔·布莱梅尔(Michelle Bleimeyer)作为家庭护理护士的工作被解雇了。
33岁的Bleimeyer已经在马萨诸塞州西部的这个家庭工作了大约一年，照顾一名患有染色体疾病的2岁儿童。她说，她不确定自己被解雇的确切原因，但当孩子的父母开始在家工作时，情况发生了变化。
“一个是办公室经理，另一个是会计……。我认为这是问题的一部分。我想他们都因为在家工作而有点紧张，这也是我被解雇的部分原因。“她说。
Bleimeyer在2009年获得了护理学位，她说在大衰退之后很难找到工作。“我很恐慌，因为我的助学贷款就要到期了，结果我真的很喜欢(家庭护理)，”她说。
今年早些时候，Bleimeyer还注册了助产学硕士学位课程，但大约在她被解雇的同时，她没有通过一次考试。她已经在与抑郁和焦虑作斗争了，所以她决定是时候休息一下了。她从推特上知道了OnlyFans，当她意识到自己从失业中获得的钱不足以维持生活时，她决定开一个账户。
在许多方面，这就是性工作一直以来的运作方式。“在新冠肺炎期间，非性工作者开始只使用风扇，由于失去了其他工作，他们成为了性工作者。性工作者权利倡导者凯特·陈(Kate Zen)说：“所以，你再一次看到性工作，就像它经常做的那样，是人们最后的一种工作。”“一直以来都是这样。不仅仅是在这场大流行期间。对很多人来说...。性工作不一定是他们唯一的职业，甚至不一定是他们的主要职业，而是他们在困境中可以获得的这种非正式收入的紧急来源。“。
但自2016年成立以来，OnlyFans一直是色情表演者和其他性工作者的热门替代收入来源，随着政府关门迫使人们留在家里，美国面临大规模失业，OnlyFans增长迅速。它吸引了碧昂斯和贝拉·索恩等名人的主流关注，也吸引了更多像布莱迈尔和克拉拉这样以前没有做过性工作的人的关注。
该网站是一个订阅平台，允许内容创建者发布付费照片和视频，与订阅者聊天，并接收提示。虽然该网站上的内容并不全是色情内容，但这是它最广为人知的地方。
Bleimeyer对自己生活中的起起落落毫不掩饰和坦率，她说，到目前为止，经历比她预期的要好。她发布内衣照片、裸照和一些视频，当她通过短信与订阅者聊天时，她说他们比她想象的更有礼貌。
“事实上，他们在发照片之前会先问一下。这是一次非常愉快的经历，“她说。“这是我最吃惊的地方。”
虽然性工作的概念本身对许多用户来说可能是新的，但克拉拉和其他人表示，在网站上发布内容并不是一个巨大的飞跃，因为内容与他们已经在Twitter或Instagram上发布的内容没有太大不同。
32岁的米娅今年4月失去了南卡罗来纳州一家疗养院注册护士助理的工作，当时她已经怀孕8个月。她说，从她已经发布的照片中赚钱似乎是显而易见的。

“现在的计划是从cna(注册护士助理)到lpn(有执照的执业护士)，再到rn(注册护士)…。你知道，生活阻碍了我，我有几个孩子，工作日程可能要求很高，“她说。“我从十几岁起就一直想当一名护士，但从来没有花时间去应聘。”

推广工作不是一件容易的事。OnlyFans没有让观众发现新账户的主页，所以内容创建者必须建立追随者，并通过其他社交媒体账户做广告。

“预期的答案是，OnlyFans正在成为主流，我认为这部分是正确的，”伯格通过电子邮件告诉BuzzFeed新闻，回答有关该平台越来越多的接受程度的问题。“但知名度的提高带来了更强烈的抵制，因为它得到了更多的主流接受。我们看到，来自Incels的网络骚扰越来越多，来自反性工作者、女权主义者和保守派的担忧也在不断增加。
Bleimeyer说，在她开设OnlyFans账户之前，她曾在网上遭遇过骚扰，但现在情况变得明显更糟了。
Bleimeyer说：“这不同于仅仅在网上做一个女性，因为这本身就是一种特殊的骚扰。”“你很快就会打折，有些人会马上把你打折。”
“这让我很生气，”克拉拉说。作为一名病人护理助理，她说：“我看到人们死去，我看到人们无法呼吸。我看到同事们在想，我们什么时候才能有更多的人工作，因为工作量变得如此之大。这一切我都看过了，当人们试图让我失望时，他们会说，‘哦，你为什么要说话，你什么都不知道。’这简直是疯了。实际上就像是，‘伙计，我喜欢。’我知道的很多。“。
虽然Bleimeyer对她在OnlyFans上的工作是公开的--“如果我要当助产士，那也差不多”--但接受BuzzFeed新闻采访的其他女性表示，她们觉得自己必须至少对工作中的某些内容保密，因为她们担心工作中的评判或后果。
“进入护理行业和只做粉丝并不是一个很好的组合。你会为此惹上麻烦的，“尼基说，并补充说她认为有可能会被吊销驾照。“我对此非常谨慎。如果我去上学，付了这么多钱，我可不想被吊销驾照。“。
对克拉拉来说，这段经历造成了她与同学之间的距离，她说这很难克服。“我觉得自己和很多学生是隔绝的。我觉得如果他们发现了--很多人都知道，他们也没意见--但我觉得如果其他人知道了，他们会对我产生不好的印象。“。
Zen说，现在断言越来越多的网络性工作者是否会对性工作者的权利有任何意义还为时过早。她还告诫说，在OnlyFans的背景下，这样的讨论是有限的，因为他们关注的是一种合法形式的性工作，但她认为人们更公开地使用“性工作者”这个词是有希望的。
克拉拉再次回到医院工作，在担任患者护理助理的同时还在做性工作，但在上周与一名患者共事后，她的新冠肺炎检测呈阳性，在她和男友康复期间，她再次被困在家里。
“仅仅因为我是一个基本上是一个摄像机女孩的人，并不意味着我比其他任何人都配得上更少的东西。人们不能再这么吹毛求疵了。我们现在正处于困难时期。人们选择的养活自己的方式是他们自己的事，“她说。
“如果它没有伤害到你，那为什么这有什么大不了的呢？”
